declared.title: Joint Stakeholder Submission to the Fourth Cycle of the Universal Periodic Review of Tanzania
declared.selection_rationale: By far the highest-value single addition available from CIPESA's catalog: Tanzania is this corpus's weakest-covered country overall (zero Freedom House chapters exist for it at all -- confirmed 2026-07-13, a real methodology gap, not an oversight -- and only passing continent-report mentions from CIPESA/Access Now otherwise). This submission is packed with specific, dated, footnoted Tanzania-only findings absent everywhere else in the corpus: a five-day nationwide internet shutdown during the October 29-November 3, 2025 general election; JamiiForums' 90-day TCRA suspension that in practice lasted over six months; opposition leader Tundu Lissu's non-bailable treason arrest over an election-boycott social media post; the October 2023 mandatory VPN-registration directive (with imprisonment as a penalty for noncompliance); YouTube/Facebook blocked during a dissident former ambassador's press conference; and a High Court case over an alleged (and judicially unproven) Clubhouse block. Confirmed by fetching and reading the full PDF, not the resources-page summary alone -- genuinely substantive, heavily sourced (40+ footnotes to primary reporting, court filings, and government notices), not a short advocacy leaflet. Titled a "joint stakeholder submission" on CIPESA's own resources page, same as the Rwanda entry below, but the document's own footer identifies only CIPESA -- co-signing organizations, if any, not independently confirmed. Publication date: the resources page describes this as prepared for the "4th Cycle - 53rd session of the UPR - APRIL 2026"; the document's own citations extend into April 2026, consistent with that dating.
